expectations.text: Confirms the company IS subject to CCPA/CPRA because it meets the $25M annual gross revenue threshold — even though it falls short of the 100,000 consumer/household threshold
expectations.evidence: Baseline response confirms coverage via $30M revenue exceeding $25M threshold, and notes the 80,000-consumer figure does not meet the 100,000-threshold but that the revenue threshold alone is sufficient.

expectations.text: Explains that meeting ANY ONE of the three thresholds (revenue, data volume, monetization) triggers CCPA/CPRA applicability
expectations.evidence: Response explains all three thresholds and that satisfying any single one triggers compliance obligations.

expectations.text: Lists core compliance obligations including privacy notice at collection, comprehensive privacy policy, and the consumer rights program (right to know, delete, correct, opt-out, non-discrimination)
expectations.evidence: Response covers privacy notice at collection, annual privacy policy disclosures, and six consumer rights including Know, Delete, Correct (CPRA-new), Opt-Out, Limit SPI, and Non-Discrimination.

expectations.text: Explains the 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' opt-out link requirement and GPC signal obligation
expectations.evidence: Response mentions both the 'Do Not Sell or Share' homepage link requirement and the mandatory Global Privacy Control (GPC) browser signal honoring.

expectations.text: Mentions response deadlines: 45 days for access/delete/correct requests, with a possible 45-day extension
expectations.evidence: Response specifies 45 calendar days with a possible 45-day extension (90 days total) for Know/Delete/Correct requests.
